'''War-robots''' or '''war droids''' were a type of combat automaton used by [[Xim/Legends|Xim the Despot]] during his military campaigns. One of the first known combat [[Droid/Legends|droids]] used in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], the war-robots were heavily armed and [[Armor/Legends|armored]] and used to subjugate entire worlds by their master.

==Description==
[[File:XimsWarRobot_egtd.png|thumb|left|180px|War-robot schematics.]]
War-robots were bipedal [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Droid/Legends|droids]] that were larger than [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]].<ref name="EGD" /> [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] technology, appropriated from the castoffs who survived in the [[Tion Hegemony/Legends|Tion Hegemony]], was used in the construction of the droids, allowing them a degree of self-awareness that was far beyond the contemporary droid designs of the day. War-robots could interact with their creators although the use of a primitive [[Behavioral circuitry matrix/Legends|behavioral circuitry matrix]] meant that the droids rigidly and literally interpreted the orders given to them.<ref name="NEGD" /> War-robots possessed limited independent reasoning, and received orders from controllers who manned the [[transmission horn]]s installed on military command podiums.<ref name="EGD" />

Covered with thick [[Armor/Legends|armor]] plating coated with a mirrored reflective surface known as [[Kiirium/Legends|kiirium]] for deflecting enemy fire and reinforced stress points, it was difficult for an enemy to damage one of the droid's vital systems.<ref name="EGD" /> However, the heavy armor made the droids slow and stiff while moving.<ref name="NEGD" />

Shielded apertures built into their arms and hands contained a variety of chemical and energy weapons, including ancient [[heatbeam]]s and [[particle discharger]]s. Their heavy cranial turrets were adorned with tiny unit insignias which carried [[Photoreceptor/Legends|optical lenses]] and a speaker.<ref name="EGD" /> However, the droid's power delivery system was considered to be inefficient.<ref name="Guide">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe]]''</ref>

War-robots ranks could be determined by the color of the death's-head emblem on their breastplates. The rank-and-file, known as "[[Corpsman]]," had a white death's head. [[Corps Commander]]s,<ref name="NEGD" /> who were designed with greater independence<ref name="EGD" /> could be distinguished by a golden death-head emblem. The rarest variety was the [[Crimson Condottiere]], who employed [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] technology, sported a red death's-head.<ref name="NEGD" /> Despite being outdated by modern era standards, they were still deadly opponents in combat.<ref name="EGD" />

==History==
[[File:GuardianCorps-FF60.png|thumb|left|180px|One of Xim's war-robots standing to attention.]]
During his reign from [[25,130 BBY]] – [[25,100 BBY]], the [[Tionese (group)/Legends|Tionese]] dictator Xim controlled his empire through the use of the highest technology available at the time including the war-robots.<ref name="NEGD" /> The droids were considered to be the first known combat automatons deployed in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="FF60">{{FactFile|60|MIL 13-14|Military Droids}}</ref> [[Factory/Legends|Factories]] on the planets [[Jaminere/Legends|Jaminere]], [[Cadinth]], and [[Thanium]] constructed [[Legion/Legends|legions]] of war-robots for Xim's forces.<ref name="History">{{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-extra-the-history-of-xim-and-the-tion-cluster|text=Essential Atlas Extra: The History of Xim and the Tion Cluster|int=Xim Week: The History of Xim and the Tion Cluster}}</ref> Xim used the war-robots to conquer countless worlds, with entire [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] surrendering in the face of their might.<ref name="EGD" />

However, Xim's empire eventually came into contact with the [[Hutt Empire]]. Hoping to vanquish the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]] and become the master of the entire [[Si'Klaata Cluster/Legends|Si'Klaata Cluster]], he committed nearly all of his resources to the [[Xim Wars|campaign]]. However, at the [[Third Battle of Vontor]], the Hutts unleashed their new army of [[Klatooinian/Legends|Klatooinian]], [[Nikto/Legends|Nikto]], and [[Vodran (species)/Legends|Vodran]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] on Xim's [[Army/Legends|army]]. These soldiers decimated his military forces, thus ending his reign.<ref name="EGD" /> Xim was captured and died a [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]] in the dungeons of [[Kossak Inijic Ar'durv|Kossak the Hutt]] on [[Varl/Legends|Varl]].<ref name="NEGD" />

Xim's [[treasure ship]], the [[Queen of Ranroon/Legends|''Queen of Ranroon'']], escaped to the planet of [[Dellalt/Legends|Dellalt]] carrying a thousand of the war-robots,<ref name="EGD" /> known as the [[Guardian Corps]].<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|373|Guardian Corps}}</ref> Once on Dellalt, the war-robots were off-loaded to guard the [[Treasure Vaults of Xim the Despot]]. The droids were maintained by a cult known as the "[[Survivors (Dellalt)|Survivors]],"<ref name="EGD" /> who were descended from the original crew and honor guards on board the ''Queen of Ranroon''.<ref name="EC">[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']]</ref>

[[File:Ximsbigrobotguy.png|thumb|right|160px|One of the War-Robots looks down.]]
Millennia later in [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] encountered the Guardian Corps during an adventure on Dellalt. The war-robots were used by the Survivors against a group of offworlders who started a semi-legal [[J'uoch's mining camp|mining camp]] near the Survivor encampment. However, nearly all of the Corps was destroyed.<ref name="HSATLL">''[[Han Solo and the Lost Legacy]]''</ref> Later, several archaeologists and researchers including the [[Ruurian/Legends|Ruurian]] [[S. V. Skynx/Legends|S. V. Skynx]] began researching these ancient war-robots.<ref name="EGD" />

After Xim's defeat, many of the war-robots had been taken as trophies by the Hutts and placed on display in public plazas and on palace steps. Most assumed that they were actually just decorations for the [[City/Legends|cities]] the Hutts lived in. In truth, the war-robots were actually a desperate fail-safe which were to be activated in the event of an invasion of [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]]. When the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Battle of Nal Hutta|invaded]] [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]] and the rest of Hutt space in [[26 ABY/Legends|26 ABY]], the war-robots—outdated several millennia over—mowed down thousands of [[Warrior caste|Yuuzhan Vong warriors]] in their wake. The last of Xim's war-robots were finally destroyed by the invaders' [[blast bug]]s and [[amphistaff]]s.<ref name="NEGD" />
